description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a distinctive pattern of parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are evenly spaced and reveal a contrast between the lighter stone material and the deep black recesses, giving the block a striking striped appearance. The sides of the block are solid black, with no visible texture or pattern, emphasizing the visual focus on the grooved top. The stone surface appears slightly rough and speckled, suggesting a granite or concrete composition. The block is thick and robust, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its design is utilitarian, with no visible markings, engravings, or decorative elements.
description: A thick rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on top, featuring a striped pattern of light stone and deep black recesses. The sides are solid black and the surface is rough and speckled.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the solid black side face, with grooves running perpendicular to the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same horizontal grooves and striped pattern visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth of the grooves and the solid black side face.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean with a slightly rough, speckled texture.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
